core_mechanism: using an invariance/symmetry group (scale-conformal symmetry) together with a bulk-to-boundary duality to fully fix how a subregion quantity depends on the geometry of the interface bounding it, with the result decomposing into a topology-only invariant term and a separate term set by the interface's local curvature
brief_reason: The abstract fixes the dependence of the entropy on the separating surface by imposing conformal invariance and a holographic (bulk-boundary) correspondence, and shows the result splits into a piece determined purely by the surface's topology and a piece governed by its extrinsic (curvature) geometry.
